+## What
+Create the first four `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md Examples §1–4, each runnable via `swift run <Name>` and compiled in CI: **EchoTool** (~20-line hello world: stdio echo server → MCPServer → LanguageModelSession(mcp:) on the system model → one prompt, one tool call); **FileAssistant** (stdio filesystem server, multi-tool selection, isError bubbling on a missing-file prompt); **ToolPicking** (one loose MCPTool + a native Swift Tool composed in one session — MCPToolProvider flattening); **RemoteHTTP** (HTTPClientTransport + host-supplied bearer token, demonstrating the delegated-auth decision).
+
+- [ ] EchoTool
+- [ ] FileAssistant
+- [ ] ToolPicking
+- [ ] RemoteHTTP
+- [ ] All four wired into CI as build targets
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `swift build --target <Name>` succeeds for all four locally and in CI
+- [ ] Each example's source has a header comment stating what it demonstrates, matching plan.md
+- [ ] Model-dependent runtime paths guard on SystemLanguageModel availability with a clean message (examples must not crash on CI machines without the model)
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] CI job builds all four example targets (compilation is the automated gate)
+- [ ] Non-model helper logic (e.g. RemoteHTTP token injection) covered by unit tests in `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ExampleHelperTests.swift`
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kwr5fwykp7gt2xgy39bsz915
+  text: |-
+    Implementation complete, build/test green. Summary:
+
+    Created:
+    - `Examples/EchoTool/EchoTool.swift`, `Examples/FileAssistant/FileAssistant.swift`, `Examples/ToolPicking/ToolPicking.swift`, `Examples/RemoteHTTP/RemoteHTTP.swift` — four `@main`-based executable targets (not `main.swift` top-level scripts, deliberately, so the test target can `@testable import` them for helper-logic coverage).
+    - `Examples/Support/` — new `ExampleSupport` library target shared by the four examples: `ExampleServerProcess.swift` (spawns `MCPTestServerCLI` as a subprocess, mirroring `E2ETests.swift`'s Process+Pipe+StdioTransport pattern), `ExampleModelGuard.swift` (`checkSystemLanguageModelAvailable`), `ConnectedExampleServer.swift` (`connectExampleServer`/`requireExampleServer` combining guard+spawn+connect).
+    - `Sources/MCPTestServer/ServerMode.swift` (new) — `ServerMode` enum (echo/filesystem/all) with `parse(from:)` + `registerTools(on:)`, so `MCPTestServerCLI` can select its tool set via `--mode`. `.all` (default, no flag) preserves the CLI's original register-everything behavior, so `E2ETests.swift` (which spawns with no args) is unaffected.
+    - `Sources/MCPTestServerCLI/main.swift` updated to use `ServerMode.parse`.
+    - `Package.swift` updated: added `ExampleSupport` + 4 example `executableTarget`s (explicit `path:` under `Examples/`), a shared `foundationModelsLinkerSettings` constant (was 6x duplicated `.linkedFramework("FoundationModels")` literal), and added `ExampleSupport`/`RemoteHTTP` to the test target's dependencies (via SwiftPM's test-target-imports-@main-executable-target support — not `@testable import` of the fixture target).
+    - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — added a "Build Examples" step running `swift build --target <Name>` for each of the four.
+    - Tests: `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ServerModeTests.swift` (9 tests, TDD'd) and `Tests/FoundationModelsMCPTests/ExampleHelperTests.swift` (9 tests, TDD'd) covering ServerMode parsing/registration, ExampleServerProcess's executable-locating logic, the model-availability guard, `requireExampleServer`'s nil-when-unavailable branch, and RemoteHTTP's bearer-token `requestModifier`.
+
+    Design judgment calls:
+    1. **ToolPicking cannot use `LanguageModelSession(mcp:)`'s variadic init for the native tool** — that initializer only accepts `MCPToolProvider`-conforming values (only `MCPTool`/`MCPServer` conform per the completed MCPToolProvider task), and a plain `FoundationModels.Tool` doesn't conform. plan.md's illustrative `LanguageModelSession(mcp: clockTool, readFileTool)` snippet doesn't match the delivered API surface. Resolved by having ToolPicking call `resolveSessionTools(from:)` directly (the same flattening function the `mcp:` convenience wraps) on the one loose `MCPTool`, then appending the native `ClockTool` and constructing the session via the base `LanguageModelSession(tools:instructions:)` initializer — documented inline in ToolPicking.swift's header doc comment. Did not modify MCPToolProvider itself (out of scope, already reviewed/done).
+    2. **Examples use `@main` structs, not `main.swift` top-level scripts** — required so the test target can import them for `ExampleHelperTests.swift`/testable coverage of non-model helper logic, per SwiftPM's "test targets can depend on @main executable targets" support.
+    3. **`ExampleServerProcess` marked `@unchecked Sendable`** (wraps Foundation's non-Sendable `Process`) and `ConnectedExampleServer: Sendable` — needed to satisfy Swift 6 strict concurrency when calling the aggregate's own async `shutdown()` method from a `defer` in `@main`'s isolated context; each use is self-contained (no concurrent shared mutation), so `@unchecked` is safe.
+    4. Added a shared `requireExampleServer` combining the model-availability guard with spawn+connect after an initial review pass flagged the guard and connect-plumbing as 3x-duplicated across EchoTool/FileAssistant/ToolPicking.
+
+    Verification commands run (all green):
+    - `swift build` (clean, zero warnings)
+    - `swift build --target EchoTool` / `FileAssistant` / `ToolPicking` / `RemoteHTTP` (all succeed individually)
+    - `swift test` → 196/196 tests pass, 22 suites
+    - `mcp__sah__review review working` → 0 findings after two fix rounds (first round flagged duplicated model-guard/spawn-connect logic across the three subprocess examples plus the linker-literal duplication in Package.swift; both fixed)
+    - Adversarial double-check agent dispatched for final sign-off (in progress as of this comment)
+
+    Leaving task in `doing` per /implement workflow — not moving to review myself.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-05T03:34:58.259969+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KWMS1F9CC0XYB0Q446930PBX
 - 01KWMSDVP4JM77YR0YMCE9S5ME
